==Description== AB. is an abandoned indoor structural complex that appears unusually clean and may extend for several miles. All structures are constructed from wood and painted white; the paint appears to be water-based. There are no visible nail holes or shrinkage cracks in the wood anywhere within the level, including at surface joints. Most structures share a similar style, featuring objects primarily from the 2000s. While the length and width of each structure vary, the ceiling and floor heights remain constant at 2.7 meters (approximately 9 feet). The corridors within the structures are furnished, though none of the furniture obstructs passage. These pieces are typically hollow inside and free of dust. The arrangement of the furniture follows a discernible pattern, most notably with cabinets topped by table lamps. Occasionally, doors appear along the walls. These doors are made of the same material and painted the same color as the walls, and when closed, there are no visible gaps between the door and the wall. The door handles are all round knobs. There are no markings on the doors—no numbers, symbols, or text of any kind. The lighting is highly unusual. Apart from the outermost room structures, no visible light sources are present. However, moving deeper inside does not immediately result in darkness; complete darkness only occurs around the fourteenth corridor. It should be noted that wanderers always appear in the outermost room structures upon entering this level. It is currently unknown whether AB. includes a genuine outdoor space, as the structures within it cannot be damaged. The view of this outdoor area features a row of trees and an endless expanse of lush grassland. The scenery appears continuous and free of any inconsistencies. ==Entrance== The only way to enter AB. is by chance. ==Exit== Noclipping through the floor will take you to '''Stair of Building'''. Throwing a window with a piece of cookware will teleport you to '''Claustrophobia'''.

=Level 601= {{Class Variable|hexagon=🕱|safety=Totally Unsafe|security=Extreme Lethal Environment|entity=Cannot Leave|class=Deadzone}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=abbrvid.webp|hovertext=Level 60?|caption=The Image of Level 601}} Level 601, also known as “Abandoned” or “Abbr,” is a level consisting of numerous clean, 2000s-style interior structures combined together. Its total estimated area is believed to be comparable to that of Level 0 or Level 3. All known stable structural components are made from a special type of wood. This material is impervious to liquids and shows no signs of water absorption, corrosion, or mold growth. Most areas are coated with white water-based paint. The surface is smooth and evenly covered, with no visible peeling or cracks. The bond between the paint layer and the wood is exceptionally strong; even after prolonged scraping and high-frequency friction tests, the coating remains intact. The origin of the paint is unknown, and no matching samples have been found in any other known level. A large number of furniture pieces are scattered throughout the level. These contain no usable resources and are typically completely empty. Their placement and arrangement exhibit clear orderliness. Aside from occasional damage and the rare instance of fallen pieces, there is almost no variation. Proportionally, most furniture cannot be moved; however, this limitation applies only within corridors, which will be discussed later. The entire structure follows a repeating pattern of rooms and corridors. Wanderers will inevitably arrive first at the outermost room. Aside from the exterior-facing windows of this outermost room, there are no known light sources. However, light does not actually disappear until the fourteenth corridor. Even-numbered structures generally contain more furniture than odd-numbered ones, though this does not necessarily make even-numbered corridors more obstructed, as the furniture is always positioned along the sides. Doors and square openings may appear in the walls, allowing wanderers to access different structural sections. Neither type has a doorframe or visible support structure; they are simply precise cutouts in the wall. Their edges are made of the same material as the surrounding wall. In corridors, the intervals between these openings are often measured in miles, with even the shortest distances spanning several hundred meters. Additionally, square openings occur far more frequently than doors. In some respects, this place resembles a cluster of abandoned houses, arranged in an unusual and illogical manner. Due to the visually distorted nature of the environment, individuals with low psychological resilience may experience intense stress and panic, resulting in nausea, dizziness, and cognitive dissonance. This level exhibits an irreversible phenomenon: expressed language and written text gradually become shortened over time, affecting both grammar and vocabulary. Once compressed to its limit, the wanderer will disappear, along with any clothing they are carrying. Before this event occurs, wanderers are able to sense its impending onset; if alerted by something, they may become aware of it even earlier. ==Entities== No entities reside within this level. Logically, should any enter, they would inevitably become victims themselves. ==Colonies and Outposts== This level lacks the necessary conditions to establish colonies or outposts; consequently, none have ever existed. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== If you very unlucky, you will in here. ===Exits=== There's no way out anymore...
